claim: GPT-5 can reproduce peer-reviewed theoretical physics papers in 30 minutes that took expert physicists months to develop
actionable: Use GPT-5 Pro for accelerating solution discovery in theoretical physics; allocate more time to verification than generation
source_title: How GPT-5 derived new results in theoretical physics and quantum gravity — Alex Lupsasca, OpenAI

claim: GPT-5 solved a year-long unsolved physics problem (single-minus gluon amplitudes) that was puzzling expert physicists before the human collaborator even arrived
actionable: When facing long-standing unsolved problems in mathematical/physics domains, prompt GPT-5 with the problem statement and allow extended thinking time (12+ hours for complex problems)
source_title: How GPT-5 derived new results in theoretical physics and quantum gravity — Alex Lupsasca, OpenAI

claim: Extended thinking models (GPT-5.2 Pro with 20-30 minute thinking time) can discover simplifications in highly complex multi-term physics expressions by autonomous exploration of mathematical space
actionable: Enable extended thinking mode for mathematical discovery problems; the model can internally run symbolic computations on 5000+ possibilities and identify patterns humans would miss
source_title: How GPT-5 derived new results in theoretical physics and quantum gravity — Alex Lupsasca, OpenAI

claim: Using an internal OpenAI physics-optimized model with 12-hour thinking time, can independently derive mathematical proofs for conjected formulas without being spoon-fed the answer
actionable: For critical verification of AI-generated conjectures, re-prompt a fresh instance with the problem statement only (not the conjectured answer) to obtain independent derivation and proof
source_title: How GPT-5 derived new results in theoretical physics and quantum gravity — Alex Lupsasca, OpenAI

claim: ChatGPT Pro can generalize from a solved problem in one domain (gluon amplitudes) to an adjacent but mathematically different domain (graviton amplitudes) without explicit formulas provided
actionable: Seed models with well-explained solved examples in a simpler domain; ask the model to identify conceptual parallels and apply them to harder variants with different mathematical structure
source_title: How GPT-5 derived new results in theoretical physics and quantum gravity — Alex Lupsasca, OpenAI

claim: Models successfully discover and apply obscure mathematical theorems (e.g., directed matrix tree theorem) in novel physics contexts without explicit instruction
actionable: Leverage models' broad mathematical knowledge base by letting them autonomously suggest applicable theorems; expert review can validate appropriateness
source_title: How GPT-5 derived new results in theoretical physics and quantum gravity — Alex Lupsasca, OpenAI

claim: The time spent on AI-generated physics papers is now dominated by human verification rather than generation—most effort goes to checking correctness, not deriving results
actionable: When using GPT for research generation, budget most time for verification and testing rather than prompting; develop systematic checking procedures for long mathematical derivations
source_title: How GPT-5 derived new results in theoretical physics and quantum gravity — Alex Lupsasca, OpenAI

claim: Current models excel at technical problem-solving and recombination but have not yet demonstrated superhuman ability to identify which unsolved problems are worth pursuing
actionable: Use models for execution of well-specified research questions; rely on human expertise for 'taste' in problem selection and research direction
source_title: How GPT-5 derived new results in theoretical physics and quantum gravity — Alex Lupsasca, OpenAI

claim: Models with sufficient scale can suggest the top 3 follow-up research questions that match expert human judgment about what to investigate next
actionable: After completing a research result, ask the model: 'What are the top 3 most important follow-up questions?' and cross-check against your own intuitions for research direction validation
source_title: How GPT-5 derived new results in theoretical physics and quantum gravity — Alex Lupsasca, OpenAI

claim: With proper steering and verification, current models can generate publication-quality physics papers at a rate of approximately one per day
actionable: If publishing volume is a goal, implement systematic workflows: formulate problem, generate with model (30 min–2 hours), verify (6–12 hours), write/format (2–4 hours per paper)
source_title: How GPT-5 derived new results in theoretical physics and quantum gravity — Alex Lupsasca, OpenAI

claim: GPT-5 reduced a 32-term physics expression to a 4-term product, achieving linear rather than factorial growth in complexity as particle count increases
actionable: When models simplify complex expressions, verify the asymptotic behavior of the result; linear scaling vs. factorial scaling indicates whether a fundamental simplification has been found
source_title: How GPT-5 derived new results in theoretical physics and quantum gravity — Alex Lupsasca, OpenAI

claim: Models can compress multi-step physics calculations into single prompts if given sufficient context; internal reasoning handles hundreds of implicit steps without explicit instruction
actionable: Frame physics problems as single coherent questions with full context; let the model's reasoning engine work through cascading implications rather than micromanaging step-by-step
source_title: How GPT-5 derived new results in theoretical physics and quantum gravity — Alex Lupsasca, OpenAI

claim: Researchers using AI now spend less time confused during research because the model can rapidly reconcile apparent contradictions or missing conceptual links
actionable: When hitting an intellectual roadblock, ask the model: 'I got result X but it seems to contradict Y. Help me reconcile these.' This accelerates the path to deeper understanding
source_title: How GPT-5 derived new results in theoretical physics and quantum gravity — Alex Lupsasca, OpenAI

claim: Running multiple parallel AI 'scouts' exploring different solution strategies simultaneously can identify promising approaches faster than sequential human exploration
actionable: For complex problems, spawn 5–10 parallel ChatGPT instances with different problem framings; evaluate which approaches show promise before committing deep human effort
source_title: How GPT-5 derived new results in theoretical physics and quantum gravity — Alex Lupsasca, OpenAI

claim: Collaboration skills with humans transfer directly to interaction skills with advanced LLMs: knowing what problem to give, at what level of detail, and when to let the assistant work independently
actionable: Improve your AI collaboration by treating models like skilled junior colleagues: practice giving well-scoped questions, providing context without over-constraining, and knowing when to step back
source_title: How GPT-5 derived new results in theoretical physics and quantum gravity — Alex Lupsasca, OpenAI

claim: LLMs trained on middle-of-the-road solutions naturally suppress sampling from the tails of the distribution; for novel research, you must explicitly prompt for unconventional or speculative approaches
actionable: When seeking creative breakthroughs, use prompts like: 'Ignore conventional wisdom. What are three outlandish but internally consistent approaches to this problem?'
source_title: How GPT-5 derived new results in theoretical physics and quantum gravity — Alex Lupsasca, OpenAI

claim: The informal-chat format of model interactions can surface insights faster than formal mathematical presentation; conversational back-and-forth allows course-correction and Socratic exploration
actionable: Use ChatGPT's multi-turn interface iteratively rather than trying to formulate the perfect prompt upfront; treat it like a working session with a colleague
source_title: How GPT-5 derived new results in theoretical physics and quantum gravity — Alex Lupsasca, OpenAI

claim: Static PDF papers may become obsolete in favor of interactive AI-augmented documents where readers can ask clarifying questions and drill down to arbitrary detail levels
actionable: When publishing research results, consider supplementing PDFs with live chat interfaces or AI-powered interactive documents that readers can query
source_title: How GPT-5 derived new results in theoretical physics and quantum gravity — Alex Lupsasca, OpenAI

claim: Verification of AI-derived proofs may benefit from formal proof systems (Lean) as verification volume scales; informal natural-language verification becomes bottlenecked
actionable: For large-scale AI-assisted theorem proving, invest in formal verification tools like Lean to automate proof checking rather than relying on manual review
source_title: How GPT-5 derived new results in theoretical physics and quantum gravity — Alex Lupsasca, OpenAI

claim: When introducing AI-solved research results, minimize prominence of AI role in public communications; let the physics result stand on its own merit rather than sensationalizing the AI aspect
actionable: Write papers and abstracts to emphasize scientific contribution; mention AI method only in methods sections; save detailed AI discussion for supplementary blog posts or Q&A
source_title: How GPT-5 derived new results in theoretical physics and quantum gravity — Alex Lupsasca, OpenAI
